title: Type-system-first safety is underemphasized
summary: The skill did not push hard enough toward value classes, invariant wrappers, parsed models, and typed state.

source.summary: User requested stronger abstraction patterns, value classes, ADTs, and immutability.

suggested_eval.prompt: Review the Kotlin design in `evals/files/raw-primitives-and-parse-boundary.md`. Recommend how to reshape it so the type system becomes the primary safety mechanism, not comments, booleans, or repeated checks.
suggested_eval.expected_output: Guidance that introduces meaningful value types or wrappers, parses once at the boundary, and stops raw nullable primitives from leaking inward.
expectations: Recommends meaningful value classes, invariant wrappers, or parsed models for raw primitives that carry domain meaning.
Pushes normalization and validation to the boundary instead of repeating checks in core logic.
Collapses invalid nullable or boolean combinations into a typed concept instead of preserving a hidden state machine.

title: ADTs and immutability are underweighted
summary: The skill did not push strongly enough toward sealed ADTs, exhaustive branching, immutable state, and higher-order composition.

source.summary: User requested stronger ADT, immutability, and advanced Kotlin pattern guidance.

suggested_eval.prompt: Review the Kotlin design in `evals/files/sealed-adt-and-immutability.md`. Recommend how to model the behavior so callers branch on explicit variants and immutable state instead of booleans, nullable flags, and mutable accumulators.
suggested_eval.expected_output: Guidance that favors sealed ADTs, exhaustive `when`, immutable data, and confined mutation over ad hoc flags and mutable state.
expectations: Recommends a sealed hierarchy or another explicit ADT for the closed set of states or outcomes.
Calls for immutable models and confined mutation instead of public or cross-boundary mutable state.
Uses Kotlin-native expression or higher-order style when it makes the state transition or transformation clearer.

title: Deep modules are underemphasized
summary: The skill did not push back strongly enough on shallow helper clusters and broad public seams.

source.summary: User requested deep-module guidance influenced by improve-codebase-architecture.

suggested_eval.prompt: Review the Kotlin design in `evals/files/deep-module-refactor.md`. Recommend how to restructure it so callers learn a smaller interface and the workflow lives inside one deeper module.
suggested_eval.expected_output: Guidance that pulls orchestration behind a smaller interface, rejects hypothetical seams, and makes the module interface the test surface.
expectations: Names the current problem as a shallow module or helper cluster that leaks workflow coordination into callers.
Recommends keeping the workflow behind a smaller interface instead of introducing more thin interfaces or pass-through helpers.
Treats the module interface as the test surface rather than optimizing for helper-level tests.

title: Nesting over prefix soup is underemphasized
summary: The skill did not push strongly enough toward one root declaration per file, nested variants, and scope-carried naming instead of repeated prefixes.

source.summary: User requested stronger guidance around nesting, repeated prefixes, public top-level helpers, and one root declaration per file.

suggested_eval.prompt: Review the Kotlin design in `evals/files/nesting-over-prefixes.md`. Recommend how to reshape it so names stay short without repeated prefixes, and the file has one root declaration instead of several sibling roots.
suggested_eval.expected_output: Guidance that uses a single owning root with nested declarations, rejects repeated disambiguation prefixes, and avoids public top-level helpers when they belong to the owning type.
expectations: Calls out repeated prefixes and several sibling root declarations as the core smell.
Recommends one owning root declaration with nested variants, helpers, or companion-owned APIs where appropriate.
Avoids preserving multiple flat root types or public top-level helper functions just because each piece is small.
